Interface Validator<T>

Type Parameters:
T - The type of the value.
public interface Validator<T>
Validates a value that the user gave.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static Validator<Integer>
    bound(int min, int max, String errorMessage)
    Get a validator that ensures an int is within a range.
    static Validator<Integer>
    nonNegative(String errorMessage)
    Get a validator that ensure a value is not negative.
    static Validator<String>
    notBlank(String errorMessage)
    Get a validator that ensures a string is not blank.
    static <T> Validator<T>
    oneOf(Set<T> items, String errorMessage)
    Get a validator that ensure a value is in a set.
    static <T> Validator<T>
    pass()
    Get a validator that does nothing.
    void
    validate(T value)
    Validates the user value.

  • Method Details

    • pass

      static <T> Validator<T> pass()
      Get a validator that does nothing.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The type this validator is for.
      Returns:
      A validator that does nothing.

    • bound

      static Validator<Integer> bound(int min, int max, String errorMessage)
      Get a validator that ensures an int is within a range.
      Parameters:
      min - The minimum int inclusive.
      max - The maximum int inclusive.
      errorMessage - The message to show on failure.
      Returns:
      The validator.

    • notBlank

      static Validator<String> notBlank(String errorMessage)
      Get a validator that ensures a string is not blank.
      Parameters:
      errorMessage - The message to show on failure.
      Returns:
      The validator.

    • oneOf

      static <T> Validator<T> oneOf(Set<T> items, String errorMessage)
      Get a validator that ensure a value is in a set.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The type of the value.
      Parameters:
      items - The set of valid items.
      errorMessage - The message to show on failure.
      Returns:
      The validator.

    • nonNegative

      static Validator<Integer> nonNegative(String errorMessage)
      Get a validator that ensure a value is not negative.
      Parameters:
      errorMessage - The message to show on failure.
      Returns:
      The validator.

    • validate

      void validate(T value) throws PromptException
      Validates the user value.
      Parameters:
      value - The value.
      Throws:
      PromptException - If validation failed.
